mechanism_narrative: A persistently low FEDFUNDS rate signals continued policy accommodation, which supports risk assets and keeps long-end yields from falling sharply over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest when CPIAUCSL is not accelerating, because inflation pressure would otherwise force a higher term premium.
regime_hint: easing or near-zero policy rate regime
failure_condition: Reject if low-FEDFUNDS observations are followed by negative 20d equity returns more than 60% of the time, or if the signal loses directional accuracy after controlling for CPIAUCSL trends.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is rising quickly while FEDFUNDS remains unusually low, real rates become more negative and the market prices future policy normalization. That combination should lift the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days as inflation and rate expectations reprice upward.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises more than 0.3% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS stays below 0.50%
failure_condition: Reject if the trigger condition is met but 20d yield direction is negative in most cases, or if the interaction term does not outperform either proxy alone in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates labor-market slack and weaker expected demand, which lowers inflation pressure and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield down over the next 20 trading days. The signal is strongest when PAYEMS is also declining, confirming that unemployment reflects genuine labor deterioration rather than one-off noise.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises at least 0.2 percentage points over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment spikes are not followed by lower 20d Treasury yields, or if the joint UNRATE-PAYEMS signal has no incremental explanatory power versus UNRATE alone.

mechanism_narrative: Higher INDPRO signals stronger real activity and tighter capacity conditions, which raise inflation expectations and term premium. Over the next 5 trading days, this should push the 10-year Treasury yield upward when production gains are broad and not offset by a simultaneous rise in unemployment.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO changes fail to precede higher 5d yield direction, or if the effect disappears once UNRATE is included as a control.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in RSAFS captures stronger retail demand and faster nominal spending, which should lift inflation expectations and raise the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism fails if sales improve only because prices rise while real activity remains weak, since that would not support a durable yield increase.
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive RSAFS gains are not associated with higher 20d yield direction, or if the signal vanishes after adjusting for CPIAUCSL inflation changes.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in HOUST indicates a demand slowdown in housing and a weaker growth outlook, which should reduce the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 60 trading days. The effect strengthens when GDPC1 is already below trend, because housing weakness then confirms broader recession risk.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% from its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if sharp housing declines are not followed by lower 60d yield direction, or if the effect reverses when GDPC1 is above trend.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth remains positive while PAYEMS and RSAFS both improve, the economy is broadening rather than stalling, which should lift the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 60 trading days. This is a true interaction signal because the yield response depends on simultaneous strength in output, payrolls, and spending.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 is positive year-over-year and PAYEMS rises month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if the combined growth condition does not predict higher 60d yield direction, or if one proxy alone explains the result as well as the joint signal.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-unemployment regime, additional weakness in PAYEMS reinforces recession pricing and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield down over the next 20 trading days. The signal is regime-conditional because payroll declines matter most when UNRATE is already elevated and the market is discounting persistent slack.
failure_condition: Reject if payroll weakness fails to coincide with lower 20d yield direction specifically in high-UNRATE periods, or if the effect is absent outside recessionary labor regimes.
